what_it_does: Documents the DNA identity area for union synthesis: what it is, its principles, architecture, outputs, seams to other areas, and history.
observations: Frames DNA as the IDENTITY area: a generative compositional language expressed visually and verbally.
Defines identity as a navigable parameter space with dials rather than a fixed look.
Lists load-bearing principles including relativistic identity, derive-never-place, meaning-as-difference, signal economy, equal-lightness zoning, and composition rules.
Describes dna/*.json registries as source of truth feeding build-time CSS vars, proof derivation, runtime resolving, archetype renderers, and SVG organisms.
States that build-time and runtime resolve from identical math.
Describes the gallery contents as output specimens and a review surface, not a generation surface.
Identifies seams to factory generation, surface-app rendering, company data consumption, and the archetype catalog boundary.
Records a history from ConceptV pitch deck extraction through parametric dials, token grammar, live composition, and archetype renderers.
standouts: Registry-is-truth architecture connects JSON registries to build-time, proof, and runtime paths.
The file treats identity as relational and derivational rather than authored case by case.
The seam language separates theming/rendering, factory-defined archetype structure, and data consumption.
conventions: Uses manifesto-style principle names with explanatory prose.
Mixes architectural inventory, design doctrine, history, and integration notes in one command document.
Uses emphatic labels, quoted phrases, and parenthetical context to preserve intent.
Represents architecture with an ASCII tree and concise endpoint notes.
Marks existence and gaps explicitly with terms like exists, unbuilt, and gap.
concerns: Several terms are highly local to the project and not defined inside the file, such as union, factory, bridge, and address spine.
The document combines descriptive architecture with intent/history, so extraction may need field separation.
Some claims use approximate counts, such as ~20 registries, ~63 pieces, and 152 commits.
notes: Brief this file as the DNA identity synthesis ledger: principles, architecture, outputs, integration seams, and origin story.
questions: What exactly is included in the union beyond the areas named here?
What concrete factory contract is expected for consuming DNA registries and renderers?
Which registries make up the ~20 dna/*.json faces?
What are the exact boundaries between DNA-owned rendering and factory-owned archetype definitions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a self-scan of DNA's identity principles, shape, outputs, seams, and history for union synthesis.
apparent_themes: boundary mapping between identity, factory, gallery, and company data
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve the conceptual and architectural role of DNA so it can be fused with other system areas without flattening its design logic into implementation details.
novelty: A design identity system is described as a machine-readable, derivational language with live dials, verbal and visual expression, and shared math across build-time, proof, and runtime.
contribution: Contributes a ledger entry for the identity substrate: relational design laws, derivation rules, dial-driven token grammar, registry architecture, render/theming contracts, specimen gallery role, and named seams for generated archetypes and live data.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a synthesis document for the DNA identity area, describing its relational design principles, JSON registry architecture, build/proof/runtime flow, specimen gallery outputs, union seams, and history.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to brief how DNA should be understood and carried into a fused system as a derivational visual-verbal identity language with explicit integration boundaries.
